lede.legalName: Empire Distributors, Inc. (parent: Kahn Ventures, Inc., a subsidiary of McLane Company / Berkshire Hathaway)
lede.hq: 685 Hartman Road SW, Austell, Georgia 30168 (602,000-sq-ft logistics campus, opened 2019)
lede.industry: Wholesale beverage-alcohol distribution (spirits, wine, beer, RTDs and non-alcoholic mixers)
lede.coverage: Four states — Georgia, North Carolina, Tennessee and Colorado — across roughly 14 facilities; statewide in NC (all 100 counties) and TN
lede.size: ~1,600 employees; 4,500+ brands represented; estimated annual revenue near ~$868M
lede.narrative: Founded in 1940 by Max E. Kahn — who bought Fulton Distributors and started with two trucks, eight brands and 149 accounts — Empire Distributors grew from a single Atlanta warehouse into one of the largest privately run beverage-alcohol distributors in the U.S. Southeast and Mountain West. The Kahn family has steered it across three generations: founder Max, second-generation Roger Kahn, and third-generation brothers David Kahn (President & CEO) and Michael Kahn (SVP), who bought the company in 1998. In 2010 Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway, through its McLane Company subsidiary, acquired Empire's parent Kahn Ventures, giving Empire deep-pocketed backing while the Kahn family continued to run day-to-day operations. Empire entered North Carolina in 1984, scaled into Tennessee in 2010 via Horizon Wine & Spirits, expanded west into Colorado in 2014, and consolidated its Georgia operations into a 602,000-sq-ft Austell headquarters in 2019. The company markets itself under the philosophy 'Building Brands and Developing People,' representing more than 4,500 spirits, wine, and beer brands.

facts.label: Brands of spirits, wine & beer represented

timeline.title: Max E. Kahn founds Empire
timeline.body: Max E. Kahn purchases Fulton Distributors in Atlanta, Georgia and launches the business with two trucks, a single warehouse, eight brands and 149 accounts.

timeline.title: Kahn departs for WWII
timeline.body: Max Kahn pauses his role and leaves the growing business in the hands of associates to serve in World War II, returning afterward to continue building the company.

timeline.title: Empire name established
timeline.body: The operation, which had started as Fulton Distributors, takes on the Empire Distributors name as it expands its Georgia footprint in the postwar boom.

timeline.title: Entry into North Carolina
timeline.body: Empire expands beyond Georgia for the first time, entering North Carolina through the acquisition of multiple local distributors and forming Empire NC.

timeline.title: Third-generation buyout
timeline.body: Brothers David Kahn and Michael Kahn — grandsons of founder Max Kahn — acquire the company, taking the helm of the family business and driving multi-state expansion.

timeline.title: Berkshire Hathaway / McLane acquisition
timeline.body: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway, via subsidiary McLane Company, acquires parent Kahn Ventures, making Empire and Empire NC McLane subsidiaries while the Kahn family continues to run the business.

timeline.title: Tennessee entry via Horizon Wine & Spirits
timeline.body: On December 31, 2010, Empire acquires Horizon Wine & Spirits, Tennessee's largest beverage-alcohol distributor, establishing Empire Distributors of Tennessee.

timeline.title: Delta Wholesale Liquors (Memphis)
timeline.body: Empire acquires Delta Wholesale Liquors in Memphis to deepen its West Tennessee coverage and broaden the Tennessee portfolio.

timeline.title: North Carolina goes statewide
timeline.body: Empire NC acquires Wisdom Beverage, achieving statewide coverage across all 100 North Carolina counties — from the Smoky Mountains to the Outer Banks.

timeline.title: Westward leap into Colorado
timeline.body: Empire expands into Colorado via the acquisition of Baroness Small Estates, establishing a presence in the Mountain West fine-wine and spirits market.

timeline.title: B&T Distributing & Volio Vino
timeline.body: Empire acquires B&T Distributing Company in Knoxville (consolidating its Tennessee entities) and Volio Vino, Inc., a Denver fine-wine wholesaler, forming Empire Distributors of Colorado and becoming a top-five Colorado distributor.

timeline.title: 602,000-sq-ft Austell HQ opens
timeline.body: Empire opens a new state-of-the-art 602,000-square-foot logistics and corporate headquarters facility in Austell, Georgia (685 Hartman Road SW), consolidating Georgia operations.

timeline.title: Colorado portfolio additions
timeline.body: Empire Distributors of Colorado adds new spirits including Triple Dog Irish Whiskey (statewide in Colorado from August 2023), reflecting continued growth in its newest market.

timeline.title: Premium mixers & new wine brands
timeline.body: Empire signs UK premium-mixer brand Franklin & Sons for Georgia and Colorado (January) and launches Black- and woman-owned IBest Wines in Georgia (March 1), broadening into non-alcoholic mixers and emerging wine labels.

timeline.title: McCormick / Holladay multi-state deal
timeline.body: In February 2026, Holladay Distillery and McCormick Distilling Co. name Empire as distribution partner across Colorado, Georgia and Tennessee for brands including Holladay Bourbon, Five Farms Irish Cream, Tequila Rose, Broker's Gin, 360 Vodka and Hussong's Tequila.

news.headline: Holladay Distillery & McCormick Distilling name Empire distribution partner in three states
news.summary: McCormick Distilling Co. and its Holladay Distillery announced a multi-state distribution partnership with Empire across Colorado, Georgia and Tennessee, covering Holladay Bourbon, Five Farms Irish Cream, Tequila Rose, Broker's Gin, 360 Vodka and Hussong's Tequila.

news.headline: IBest Wines launches in Georgia with Empire Distributors
news.summary: Black- and woman-owned IBest Wines debuted in Georgia through Empire on March 1, 2025, introducing a Red Blend ($38 SRP) and White Blend ($33 SRP), adding an emerging, culture-forward wine brand to Empire's Georgia book.

news.headline: Franklin & Sons expands U.S. presence with Empire partnership in Georgia and Colorado
news.summary: British premium-mixer brand Franklin & Sons (est. 1886) partnered with Empire's Georgia and Colorado operations to distribute tonic water, ginger beer, ginger ale and more, citing Empire's 'exceptional reach and commitment to quality.'

news.headline: Empire hosts 2024 Atlanta Beverage Showcase
news.summary: Empire Distributors of Georgia held its 2024 Atlanta Beverage Showcase, connecting many of its newest brands with retail and on-premise buyers across the Atlanta market.

news.headline: Triple Dog Irish Whiskey rolls out statewide in Colorado via Empire
news.summary: As part of an expansion into California, Colorado and Texas, Triple Dog Irish Whiskey named Empire its Colorado partner, with statewide availability beginning August 2023.

news.headline: Top U.S. distributors project growth amid tough conditions
news.summary: Shanken's review of leading American wine and spirits distributors framed the competitive backdrop in which regional players like Empire compete and pursue new supplier agreements.

highlights: 4,500+ total spirits, wine and beer brands represented across four states
Holladay Bourbon, Five Farms Irish Cream, Tequila Rose, Broker's Gin, 360 Vodka, Hussong's Tequila (McCormick/Holladay, 2026)
Franklin & Sons premium mixers — tonic water, ginger beer, ginger ale, club soda (GA & CO, 2025)
Broad fine-wine book in Colorado anchored by Volio Imports & Baroness Small Estates

facilities.name: Austell (Corporate HQ & Georgia DC)
facilities.note: 602,000-sq-ft logistics campus and corporate headquarters, opened 2019; President (GA) Brian Graeser.

facilities.name: North Carolina branches
facilities.location: Charlotte, Raleigh, Greensboro, Asheville, Wilmington, NC
facilities.note: Five NC branches giving statewide coverage across all 100 counties.

facilities.name: Nashville (Empire of TN HQ)
facilities.note: Tennessee headquarters; President Patrick Stephenson. Branches in Memphis, Chattanooga & Knoxville.

facilities.name: Denver (Empire of CO)
facilities.note: Colorado headquarters and distribution center; President David Beyer.

pullQuotes.text: We are excited to welcome McCormick Distilling Company's top-notch brands to the Empire Colorado family.
Empire's exceptional reach and commitment to quality make them an ideal partner as we continue to grow in the U.S. market.
pullQuotes.cite: Keith Beattie, International Sales Director, Franklin & Sons (Jan 2025)

people.name: Max E. Kahn
people.meta: Bought Fulton Distributors in 1940; started Empire with two trucks, eight brands, 149 accounts; served in WWII. Deceased.

people.name: Roger Kahn
people.meta: Second-generation Kahn who led and grew Empire before the 1998 transition to his sons.

people.name: David Kahn
people.meta: Grandson of founder Max Kahn; acquired the company with brother Michael in 1998; oversees company-wide operations.

people.name: Michael Kahn
people.meta: Grandson of founder Max Kahn; co-acquired Empire in 1998; senior executive across the multi-state operation.

executives.name: Jim Schwarzkopf
executives.meta: Accounting degree, University of Northern Iowa; joined Empire in 1986.

executives.name: Gary Wolfe
executives.meta: 10 years at E&J Gallo and 3 at Johnson Brothers before joining the Kahn Ventures network.

executives.name: Bill MacPhail
executives.meta: 30+ years in IT, specializing in product and software development.

executives.name: Brian Graeser
executives.meta: Runs Georgia operations from the Austell HQ.

flags.description: Empire is family-run by the Kahn brothers but is ultimately owned by McLane Company, a wholly owned Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary (parent Kahn Ventures acquired 2010). Strategic and capital decisions sit under a large parent.

Loss of a single major supplier (Diageo, Bacardi, Pernod Ricard, etc.) can materially reshape a distributor's book — as Diageo's 2020 departure from the similarly named Empire Merchants of NY illustrated. Supplier-realignment risk applies to Empire Distributors' markets as well.

Multiple unrelated firms share the 'Empire' name (Empire Merchants NY, etc.). Care is needed to avoid attributing their news to the Kahn-family Empire Distributors of Atlanta. Official site is empiredist.com (not .org).
